title: HATE P0a/P0b implementation readiness gate

assumptions.text: Initial implementation scope is HATE-MVP-001 through HATE-MVP-003.
assumptions.impact_on_coverage: P0b QEG export is now covered by a partial-export gate; P1a/P1b are covered by later advisory gates.

assumptions.text: Shipyard-cp runtime API integration is not implemented by HATE.
assumptions.impact_on_coverage: P1b emits local advisory evidence and does not dispatch live Shipyard runtime state.

reasons: P0a specification, schemas, golden fixture, executable CLI, and Shipyard advisory evidence exist.
P0a DQ negative fixtures reproduce expected hard_dq behavior.
P0a implementation completion is Go for the local-first slice.
P0b QEG export exists as a partial export with visible missing execution, risk debt, and manual-bb bridge.
P1a trust hardening and P1b workflow mapping exist as advisory evidence.
P2/P3 product readiness exists as advisory evidence and downgrades to conditional when doctor findings or unverified acceptance remain.
Hosted SaaS runtime availability must not be claimed from local artifacts.

blocking_risks.scenario: Local advisory artifact implementation completion is mistaken for hosted SaaS runtime availability.
P0b partial export gaps are mistaken for complete QEG coverage.

waivers.containment: Use local advisory shipyard-run-evidence and verify live runtime refs in Shipyard-cp if needed.

required_follow_up: Implement dashboard/API/connector runtime before claiming hosted SaaS availability.
Keep live Shipyard runtime dispatch outside HATE unless Shipyard-cp owns the run/audit refs.
